newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. Only housekeeping: June 3 DEF 14A proxy (virtual annual meeting July 31, CEO pay +8.5%, request to add 3.5M shares to LTIP), a new prosumer/pro audio-converter product family (not AI/data-center relevant), a Norges Bank 13F position disclosure, and a small June 2 insider sale. The Apple Face ID IC design-win narrative (~$2 ASP/iPhone, 2028-29 ramp) predates June 6 and is already in the prior case. Today's -2.69% is drift after a +34.7% YTD run, not a fundamental event.

sources.label: StockTitan - Cirrus Logic DEF 14A: virtual 2026 meeting, 3.5M-share incentive plan boost
Yahoo Finance - Does Cirrus Logic's Apple design win reframe its customer concentration narrative?
StockAnalysis.com - CRUS overview, FY2026 results and analyst ratings

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already flagged CRUS for removal on weak direct HBM/advanced-packaging/CPO relevance; no new information since June 6 and a -2.7% drift day do not change that setup, so hold at the bottom of the list while the removal case stands.
verdicts.bull: Apple Face ID IC design win (~$2 ASP per iPhone, 2028-29 ramp) plus 22V/PSoC content expansion could broaden the story beyond audio codecs and re-rate the multiple.
verdicts.neutral: Solid mixed-signal franchise with FY2026 revenue of $2.0B (+5.3%) and earnings +25%, but its edge/audio exposure is peripheral to the AI compute bottleneck this page ranks.
verdicts.bear: Apple concentration (~85%+ of revenue), +34.7% YTD already pricing in the design-win optionality, and essentially no HBM/packaging/optics leverage leave little residual alpha on a bottleneck-criticality list.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete content-gain disclosures for next-gen iPhone (camera controller, Face ID IC) or an edge-AI socket win outside Apple.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Apple insourcing or socket loss; consumer handset cycle softness hitting the single-customer revenue base.

verdicts.rankRationale: On a CPO/optical alpha lens CRUS has effectively zero data-center optical exposure; yesterday's removal recommendation stands and nothing since June 6 (proxy filing, audio-converter launch) touches the CPO thesis, so hold near the bottom pending removal.
verdicts.bull: If the list retains diversified mixed-signal names, CRUS's power/converter expertise and Face ID win give it cheap optionality at ~17x earnings versus optical pure plays.
verdicts.neutral: FY2026 execution was fine (+25% earnings) but the company sells into smartphones and prosumer audio, not co-packaged optics, transceivers, or data-center interconnect.
verdicts.bear: Low/no CPO relevance, customer concentration risk, and a +34.7% YTD run mean it consumes a ranking slot a genuine optical bottleneck name should hold; residual alpha here is near zero.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any announced data-center or optical-adjacent product line (none currently indicated).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued irrelevance to the CPO theme makes the position dead weight; Apple concentration adds idiosyncratic downside.
